questionText: The temperature, $X\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$, inside a refrigerator is modelled by $X \sim N(4, 0.6^2)$. Find the interval, symmetric about the mean, within which the temperature lies $99\%$ of the time. Give the endpoints to $3$ significant figures.

steps.explanation: The temperature is normal with mean $\mu = 4\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ and standard deviation $\sigma = 0.6\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$. We want a central interval about $4$ holding $99\%$ of readings.

steps.description: Split the outer 1% between the two tails
steps.explanation: The central $99\%$ is symmetric, so the remaining $1\%$ splits equally, leaving $0.005$ in each tail.

steps.description: Find the cumulative probability at the upper endpoint
steps.explanation: Everything except the upper $0.5\%$ tail lies below the upper endpoint, so the area to its left is $0.995$.

steps.description: Use the inverse normal to find the z-value
steps.explanation: The standardised value with $99.5\%$ of the area below it is $2.576$, the standard $z$ for a central $99\%$ interval.

steps.description: Write the endpoints as mu plus or minus z sigma
steps.explanation: By symmetry the endpoints sit at $z = \pm 2.576$, that is, the same distance $z\sigma$ each side of the mean $4$.
Multiplying the $z$-value by $\sigma = 0.6$ converts it back to degrees Celsius, giving a half-width of $1.545\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$.
Subtracting the half-width from the mean gives the lower boundary of the temperature interval.
Adding the half-width to the mean gives the upper boundary.

steps.description: State the interval to 3 significant figures
steps.explanation: So $99\%$ of the time the fridge temperature is between $2.45\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ and $5.55\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$, a narrow band centred on $4\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The temperature lies in $2.45\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C} < X < 5.55\ ^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ for $99\%$ of the time (endpoints to 3 s.f.).
